explanation: Normal system activity: cleanmgr.exe system maintenance, Office document edits with normal size deltas, backup zip creation. No suspicious entropy, no extension changes, no network anomalies. All processes have legitimate parents.

Benign activity: browser downloads from legitimate IPs (Google 142.250.x), Windows Defender scan completed with no threats (Event 1001), Office apps with explorer parent, cleanmgr system maintenance. All files have normal entropy and expected content types.

Benign activity: Office document editing (WINWORD, POWERPNT) with small size deltas consistent with saves, Windows Defender scan completed clean (Event 1001), cleanmgr system maintenance creating temp files. Normal document entropy, no extension changes, no suspicious network activity.

Exfiltration-first ransomware pattern detected. Massive outbound data transfers (7.5MB, 1.2MB, 1.4MB, 0.7MB, 8.7MB, 9.3MB, 5.3MB, 8.5MB, 3.2MB, 9.9MB) to three suspicious external IPs (185.142.98.12, 45.153.241.81, 91.215.85.47) sustained across all three observation windows with escalating volume. svchost.exe engaged in network operations. Multiple document files show small modifications consistent with pre-encryption staging. This matches the exfil-first ransomware playbook: exfiltrate sensitive data before encrypting to enable double-extortion.
